title: Ancistrorhynchus glomeratus
taxonomy: Ancistrorhynchus glomeratus is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. This epiphytic orchid is native to tropical Africa and is classified as an orchid. The classification of Ancistrorhynchus glomeratus places it within the subfamily Orchidoideae and the tribe Spiranthinae. Further, it is placed in the genus Ancistrorhynchus, which is part of the Orchidaceae family. The classification of this plant is crucial for its identification and study in the field of botany.

This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ancistrorhynchus glomeratus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
